rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed enantioselective electrochemical C-H annulation of benzamides/diarylphosphinic amides with allenes to afford C-N axially chiral isoquinolinone/phosphinamide products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation selectively oxidizes Co(II) to Co(III) catalyst, enabling chiral cobaltacycle formation without chemical oxidant; Co(III)/Co(I)/Co(II) redox cycle supported by CV

key_intermediates.name: Co(III) cobaltacycle from benzamide with 4-methoxypyridine and chiral oxazoline ligand L3 (intermediate 45)
Co(III) cobaltacycle from diarylphosphinic amide with L2 and 4-methoxypyridine (intermediate 46)
Co(I) species generated after C-N bond-forming reductive elimination

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Co(II)/L complex to Co(III), enabling concerted metalation-deprotonation (CMD) C-H activation to form chiral cobaltacycle; allene migratory insertion followed by C-N reductive elimination releases product and Co(I), which is reoxidized at the anode back to Co(III)/Co(II).
rate_determining_step: Not C-H cleavage (KIE PH/PD ≈ 1.2 indicates C-H activation is not rate-determining); likely allene insertion or subsequent reductive elimination

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-benzamide substrate (d-substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Intermolecular competition gave PH/PD ≈ 1.2, indicating C-H cleavage is not the rate-determining step

probe_reagents.name: 4-methoxypyridine
probe_reagents.observation: Stabilized Co(III) cobaltacycles 45 and 46, allowing their isolation

probe_reagents.name: Isolated cobaltacycle 45
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric reaction with allene 2 + NaOPiv delivered product 3 in 52% yield, 99% ee, supporting catalytic relevance

probe_reagents.name: Isolated cobaltacycle 46
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometric reaction with allene 2 + NaOPiv gave product 31 in 41% yield, 99% ee

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Co(OAc)2/NaOPiv/L3 ± substrate 1
probe_reagents.observation: Co(OAc)2/NaOPiv shows no relevant redox; addition of L3 produces clear oxidation waves (mono- and bis-L3 Co species); reversible wave at 0.8 V vs SCE becomes less reversible upon adding substrate 1, supporting Co(III)/Co(I)/Co(II) catalytic pathway

probe_reagents.name: Thermal racemization of product 3
probe_reagents.observation: Racemization barrier 39.4 kcal/mol, t1/2 ≈ 2.7×10^8 years at 25 °C — confirms configurational stability of C-N axis

notes: 4-Methoxypyridine is used as an ancillary ligand to stabilize otherwise unstable Co(III) cobaltacycle intermediates for isolation/characterization. The authors explicitly favor a Co(III)/Co(I)/Co(II) electrocatalytic cycle in which anodic oxidation replaces a stoichiometric chemical oxidant; this is also implemented in a continuous-flow electrochemical reactor enabling decagram-scale synthesis without supporting electrolyte.
